sources.full: Drop In Boss Battles and Encounters

book.name: Drop In Boss Battles and Encounters

entries.name: Hello There!
entries: Welcome to Drop In Boss Battles and Encounters by Leopard Print Press! We have put together 20+ fights for you to use, steal, reshape, or reskin in your adventures. Whether you are using these to shorten prep time, as inspiration for your own campaigns, or as a challenge for your players, we hope that you can use the knowledge inside to help push your DND experiences to new levels with exciting new content.
Every encounter has a template that you can use for any scenario and is intended to be a buffet - grab and leave whatever you like!

entries.name: Overview
entries: The ghost of Sir Felix wants an honorable fight so that he may die and be put to rest. This fight is great for a first encounter and is "easy" as long as the characters fighting him remain "honorable." The more "dishonorable" the characters are, the harder the fight gets. This fight's goal is to introduce players to 5e and  show newer players how certain mechanics work.

entries.name: Battlefield
entries: This takes place in an arena dedicated to a god of war, honor, and/or soldiers. The arena itself is mostly in ruins with an empty center for the fight to take place.
However, you can place this fight nearly anywhere since the arena itself is not important to this battle. Common locations are a roadside, a graveyard, a cave, a field, etc.

entries.name: Fight Mechanics
entries: The fight will be less challenging should the players remain "honorable" (according to Sir Felix). "Dishonorable" actions will make Sir Felix more powerful. Below are what Sir Felix considers dishonorable; however, you may change these or add more as you see fit:

items: Using poison
Attacking from the shadows (invisibility or stealth)
Using summons
Anything that causes the stun condition

entries.name: Special Mechanics: No Death for Players
entries: When a creature goes to 0 hp, the creature goes to 1 hp instead, and does not make death saving throws.
Sir Felix cannot die until he fights every member of the party. This is to help every party member learn their class abilities and mechanics.

entries.name: Introduction
entries: {@i As you walk through the destroyed archway of the ruined arena, you hear the faint roar of a crowd off in the distance. With each step you take, the crowd grows louder and louder. The empty stands lie mostly in ruins, yet the arena itself has fresh sand - as if a new fight is about to commence. In the center of the arena is a flag, emblazoned with the crest of a golden dragon head surrounded by a rising sun.}

{@i A single horn is blown. A ghostly knight appears the arena, brandishing damaged full plate and armed with sword and shield. Emblazoned on the shield is the same crest as the flag. The knight bows.}
{@i Hello, adventurers. I am Sir Felix Kingsley, and I ask you all one favor. My soul cannot rest until I have earned a warrior's death in battle. Will you give me that honor?}

entries.name: An honorable fight
entries: Sir Felix will explain that his warrior's death must be honorable as per his god's dogma. He will expect the same from his opponents, and if they attempt to fight dirty, his god will give him the strength to fight beyond mortal means.

entries.name: Teaching a new generation of warriors
entries: Sir Felix's dogma also teaches to aid those that are learning the warrior's way, so he will also complement and give advice to his enemies to improve their fighting.

entries.name: Taking a knee
entries: If someone takes a knee, Sir Felix will not attack them until they stand back up. When a player is on their knee, they can aid their allies or heal. However, should a player abuse this and attack Sir Felix from this position, he will see it as dishonorable, and his {@b Dishonorable Action} ability will trigger (see stat block).

entries.name: Roleplaying Sir Felix
entries: During the fight, Sir Felix will try to engage with every member of the party to test their strength.
If they use good tactics, Sir Felix will compliment them and even give some advice on how to improve. Feel free to adjust the RP according to the player's class or role in the battle.

entries.name: After Felix is Defeated
entries: {@i Felix takes a knee. While holding his sword, he looks up as the skies part and a singular beam of holy light lands upon him. He stands tall as his ghostly form fades, revealing a noble human who bows and thanks everyone for granting his final wish. His form fades and the light vanishes}

items: After the events of the arena, a relative of Felix shows up and challenges the party to a contest of champions. These challenges consist of various medieval knight events such as jousting, sword fighting, etc to prove if they are worthy of the name.
More information is brought to light that Felix's soul was taken by a blackguard, who is tormenting Felix. The party must defeat the blackguard to free Felix once and for all.
Former knights who followed Sir Felix have heard what the party did and thank them. However, they need help defeating an old enemy of their knightly order.

entries.name: Reward: Seeding the Breastplate of Honor
entries: {@i Where Felix once stood, his damaged breast plate sits in the sand. A golden crest glimmers, depicting a fearsome dragon.}

entries.name: Overview
entries: The party wakes up restrained in a laboratory and must face off against an {@creature Ooze Annihilator|LPPDiBBaE} - an ooze with a brain inside! The Ooze Annihilator is slow, and the party members will need to escape before he consumes them all!

entries.name: Optional Content
entries: To make the fight more difficult, feel free to add a second Ooze Annihilator in the second tube.

entries.name: Location and Setup
entries: This encounter can take place after the party takes a long rest as they will be abducted in the middle of the night.

entries.name: If someone is taking a watch
entries: The party member on watch must make an Intelligence saving throw DC 18 or go unconscious (racial resistances, such as those offered by fey ancestry, do not work against this). The last thing they will see is a humanoid individual walking towards the party. If successful, the encounter can be avoided. However, the humanoid may come back another night to try to kidnap the party again.

entries.name: Laboratory
entries: This laboratory can be placed anywhere you want in your campaign or setting, such as the middle of nowhere or within a town in plain sight. The laboratory itself is more of a testing facility for the ooze annihilators.

entries.name: Laboratory Set Pieces
entries: The laboratory's main aspects are two tubs in the corners containing Ooze Annihilators, the main chest that contains the party's equipment, and the ooze pool in the center. Otherwise, it is a small facility that can contain other aspects of your choosing.

Next to the ooze pool are many vials that contain parts of the ooze. The ooze itself can be used as a plot point to discover more details about the laboratory or used as a Potion of Acid Resistance. It can also contain research notes on the Oozes themselves if you wish.

entries.name: Waking Up In an Unfamiliar Place
entries: The party will wake up attached to surgical tables in the laboratory with their arms and legs tied down via leather straps. All of their gear has been taken. Party members are considered {@condition Restrained} until they escape.
The party's gear is close by in the wooden chest at the end of the lab.

items: A restrained creature's speed becomes 0, and it can't benefit from any bonus to its speed.
Attack rolls against the creature have advantage, and the creature's attack rolls have disadvantage.
The creature has disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws.

entries.name: Getting Out of the Straps
entries: To get out of the straps, a party member will need to make a DC 15 Acrobatics Or Athletics check to free themselves.

entries.name: A Way Out
entries: The front door out of the laboratory is made of stone. It is also locked, and the key to the door is located in the left tube that the Ooze Annihilator is in.

entries.name: Opening the Door
entries: The party doesn't need the key to escape, but it is the easiest option.
The door can be lockpicked with Thieves tools on a DC 25 or even broken down with an Athletics check of the same DC.
Once the door has been opened, the party can walk through and escape.

entries.name: Introduction
entries: {@i You slowly wake up after a long night of sleep, but after a moment, you feel that you are in an unfamiliar place. The bubbling sounds of liquid fill your ears as you look around. You notice that this is a laboratory.}

{@i Your hands and legs are restrained, and you see your fellow party members are in the same state. The bubbling of an unknown liquid is coming from a pool in the center of the room. Green liquid fills two cylindrical tubes in the corners. Each tube contains a brain floating in the center. Between the two tubes is an unlocked wooden chest that appears to be stuffed with familiar items. There is only one visible way in and out - a locked stone door.}

entries.name: Noticing the Key
entries: With a passive perception of 14 the players can notice there is a key floating within the left tube.

{@i A loud horn echoes through the lab.  The liquid in the containers turns from green to purple. You see the left tube's glass begin to lower. The liquid doesn't spill out of the new opening... it begins to slowly ooze out. A large brain floats within the center of this ooze, and as it escapes the tube, it crawls closer to you. Fear grips you as you realize its intent... CONSUME.}

entries.name: Combat
entries: Everyone rolls initiative, with the Ooze starting its turn at the tube while the party is still restrained. The ooze is slow (move speed 20ft) but packs a powerful punch if it arrives at its target.
The ooze is single-minded in its intent and will constantly go after the nearest party member.

entries.name: Conclusion
entries: The party can choose to fight the ooze or unlock the door and make an escape.

entries.name: After Defeating the Ooze
entries: {@i Your final blow causes the mass to turn liquid. Its brain slowly pulses as its life force ebbs away. The ooze is no more, but now you must find a way to escape the laboratory.}

entries.name: Fleeing the Laboratory
entries: {@i You open the door to freedom and quickly climb a set of stone stairs. The laboratory behind you is but a distant memory of this horrific experience. You notice the laboratory was very well hidden in its current location. You think to yourself, "what other laboratories are hiding in plain sight? And worse, who was it that built this in the first place?"}

entries.name: Conclusion
entries: The party can choose to fight the ooze or unlock the door and make an escape.

entries.name: After Defeating the Ooze
entries: {@i Your final blow causes the mass to turn liquid. Its brain slowly pulses as its life force ebbs away. The ooze is no more, but now you must find a way to escape the laboratory.}

entries.name: Fleeing the Laboratory
entries: {@i You open the door to freedom and quickly climb a set of stone stairs. The laboratory behind you is but a distant memory of this horrific experience. You notice the laboratory was very well hidden in its current location. You think to yourself, "what other laboratories are hiding in plain sight? And worse, who was it that built this in the first place?"}

entries.name: More Abductions
entries: Soon, the party will hear reports of more people being abducted but not many surviving. One elf ranger does make it out alive and believes they know the location of the main lab.

entries.name: The God of Puzzles?
entries: On the road, the party will get a letter congratulating them on their escape and are told that they are chosen by the evil god of puzzles. They will be subjected to more escapes with prizes.

entries.name: Mad Science
entries: A far realm scientist has been creating new creatures and believes they can learn from the Ooze Annihilator's encounter with the party. This scientist will release a new Ooze Devourer 2.0, which now must be dealt with.

entries.caption: Details
items: {@b Tables} - This is where the characters wake up and are bound.
{@b Tanks in Bottom Left and Bottom Right} - These tubes contain the Ooze Annihilators.
{@b Wooden Crate in Bottom} - The chest that contains all of the party's equipment.
{@b Pool in the Center} - An alchemy cauldron that contains ooze contents.
{@b Door in the North Area} - The laboratory's exit.
{@b Blue Star in South West Area} - The exit key's location.

entries.name: Overview
entries: This is a twist on a a very classic encounter for adventurers... clearing rats out of a basement!
This straightforward fight can lead to possible follow ups for your campaign or setting as you see fit, and includes a cute pet companion as a reward.

entries.name: Potential Hooks
entries: The hook for this encounter can be as simple as a shopkeeper asking the party to deal with a problem in their store's basement (see {@b shopkeeper's problem}, the provided hook).
If you want to add a bit more comedy, play up the shopkeeper as an individual who severely downplays the entire situation to make the reveal of the draconic rats more amusing.
The hook can also be a job posting on the town's job board.

entries.name: A Shopkeeper's Problem
entries: Upon arriving at a store, the local shopkeeper will ask the adventurers if they are here to deal with the rat problem. The shopkeeper will explain that they keep hearing scuttering down in the basement every night, and every time they go down there to look, they are not able to find the source of the sounds. However, the shopkeeper has found evidence of rats, including rat dropping, chewed up shelves, etc. One of the things that they find odd is the basement seems much warmer than they remember. They will politely ask the party to deal with the rats, and promises that there will be a reward.

entries.name: Basement
entries: The basement itself is filled with shelves and many different obstacles that the rats can move through that the party cannot. The shelves are 10ft high, and there are various crates of different sizes throughout the basement. The rats can move under the boxes and crates without penalty. The staircase is 10ft tall and made of wood.

entries.name: Rat Eggs
entries: In a corner of the basement lies a nest containing 3 {@b Draconic Rat Eggs}. These were laid by the Draconic Rats when they decided to make this basement home.

The eggs can be used for future plot points, such as introducing an alchemist who will pay for the egg's return, or the eggs can be kept by the party and hatched at a later date (see reward: Draconic Rat Egg).

entries.name: Draconic Rats
entries: There are several rats in the basement with draconic heritage due to an alchemist's experiment. Those experiments have broken free and have made a home in the basement. The draconic rats mainly want to protect their eggs and will do so by any means necessary. If the draconic rats believe the party members are threatening the eggs in the nest, they will attack. These rats are somewhat smarter than the average rat, so their tactics are mainly defensive with a sense of self preservation as opposed to the other swarms in the basement.

entries.name: Going into the Basement
entries: {@i The shopkeeper unlocks the basement door, and immediately you hear scuttering coming from below. The shopkeeper says, "Just try not to break much" before leaving you to your work. Going down the wooden stairs, you can see many shelves filled with store products that have not yet been brought up.}

entries.name: Passive Perception of 12
entries: {@i In the corner of the basement, you see what looks like a small nest containing three scaly eggs, all red in color. Movement in the nest reveals rats that are around 2 ft in length with splotches of red scales covering their body. The rat's mouths protrude from their faces, filled with reptilian teeth.}
Upon stepping fully into the basement, the rats will reveal themselves.
{@i Emerging out of the shelves, you see several swarms of rats lunge from the darkness and attack!}
At this point, the Draconic Rats have also revealed themselves next to the nests.

entries.name: Combat
entries: The basement contains the following:

items: 4 {@creature Swarms of Rat|LPPDiBBaE}s
3 {@creature Draconic Rat|LPPDiBBaE}s

entries: The swarms of rats are will fight to defend their territory and will not stop until the adventuring party leaves.
The {@b Draconic Rats} will only get involved if the party approaches the nest. They will fight to the death to protect their eggs. If the characters go back up the stairs, the rats will not pursue.
{@b Note:} Draconic Rats will not attack {@b Dragonborn} characters unless provoked.

entries.name: Alternatives to Combat
entries: The Draconic Rats are deadly, so relocating them and their nest is also an option. See what creative ways your players can come up with, such as using {@b {@skill Animal Handling}} or {@b {@spell Speak with Animals|PHB}}.

entries.name: Conclusion
entries: After all rats are defeated or relocated, including the draconic rats, the shopkeeper will be happy. The party may find the {@b Draconic Rat Egg} in the North East rat nest. If told about the Draconic Rats, the shopkeeper will just play it off as a new weird magical rat breed or dismiss it entirely. The party will then be rewarded by the shopkeeper.

entries.title: Map of Rats... In a Basement
entries.altText: Map of Rats... In a Basement
entries.mapName: Map of Rats... In a Basement

entries.name: Overview
entries: This encounter will make the party choose between helping a crying {@b Owlbear Cub} and its mother against two (or more) hunters, or siding with the {@b hunters} to harvest the two Owlbears in order to feed their village.

items: This can be included as a random forest encounter.
{@b Pro Hunter.} The nearby village has sent hunters to gather more meat for the upcoming winter and has asked the party to assist the hunters.
{@b Pro Owlbear.} The local druids have been noticing a decrease in the owlbear population and want to hire the adventurers to find out what is going on and try to find a solution.

entries.name: A Tale of Two Sides
entries: This encounter is different because depending on your party, there is no straightforward enemy. The hunters have captured the {@b Owlbear Cub} in order to lure the mother into their trap. Owlbears provide a substantial amount of meat, bones, and feathers, which the village needs to survive. The {@b Owlbear Mother} is following its natural instincts to protect itself and its cub.
The party can also just ignore the situation entirely and let the events play out. Should this happen, the hunters will kill both Owlbears.

entries.name: Owlbear Mother Behavior
entries: The Owlbear Mother is a creature of the forest and simply trying to exist. She is angry when met due to the cries of her missing child. The Owlbear Mother will notice anyone attempting to help her child, and will not harm that person or group. However, if the intent to save the cub is not made clear to the Mother, she will attack.
The {@b Owlbear Mother} can be reasoned with, and a successful {@i DC 15 {@skill Animal Handling} check} will show her that the party means no harm and is attempting to help.
Example: If someone is freeing the Owlbear Cub and moving it to safety, a successful animal handling or other check can show her that the party means well.

entries.name: Hunters
entries: These hunters understand that the circle of life happens and creatures die. Their tactic to lure the Owlbear Mother has been taught to them by previous generations of hunters. Lives are at stake as the villagers are depending on the hunter's success.

If the adventurers want to help the hunters, they are very grateful as they understand that it will be easier to kill the Owlbear Mother with help. However, they will be very upset and angry if the party tries to help the beasts as this is a life or death situation for them.
The hunters will not fight to the death if the adventurers assist the owlbear and will flee should the fight become too difficult or the adventurers are particularly intimidating.

entries.name: Introduction
entries: {@i As you travel through the forest, the shrieks and cries of a young owl pierce the air. You follow the sounds to a small clearing, where a baby Owlbear struggles inside of a bear trap. Two hunters emerge from the brush and make themselves known. "Hey, get down! You don't want to be seen here when the mother shows up."}
{@i What do you do ?}

entries.name: The Standoff
entries: Before the mother arrives, the hunters will explain that they are hunting the baby Owlbear's mother to feed their local village. If the party wants to assist, the village will reward them. The Hunters recommend hiding to get the jump on the {@b Owlbear Mother} when she arrives.

If the {@b adventurers} attempt to free the baby, the {@b hunters} will protest and explain that they need the baby to attract the mother, and that the trap is the best way to keep it from moving. Should the adventurers continue to interfere, the hunters will attack.

entries.name: Mother's Arrival
entries: The mother's arrival can occur at any point when the characters are talking with the hunters or otherwise.

{@i The ground begins to shake as you see a large creature rapidly approaching in the distance. The hunters prepare their crossbows. "Here she comes!" They yell. Bursting through the trees, the Owlbear Mother roars and flares her feathers towards those who surround her baby.}

entries.name: Combat
entries: The hunter's main goal will be to kill the mother. They have no interest in the baby past this point.

The mother will attack whoever she sees in a fit of rage. If the players successfully attempt to help the baby or successfully attempt to calm down the mother, she won't attack them. There are many ways to attempt to calm her down, such as speak with animals or animal handling. Allow some creativity with this!
However, if the hunters will continue shooting the Owlbear mother, which will ruin any attempts at keeping her calm.
The hunters will not fight to the death and will flee if their lives feel threatened. The mother won't pursue anyone beyond the clearing.

entries.name: Hunter Victory
entries: {@i As the last bolt is shot into their quarry, the mother falls and becomes motionless. The hunter's rejoice; some begin harvesting the animal for transport while the others go back to their village for a cart to move the beast. The town will be fed for many months.}
{@i The baby is now left with no mother or way to feed itself. The hunter's do not care about the baby and tell you that their plan is to release it back into the wild. What will you do ?}

entries.name: Owlbear Victory
entries: {@i The owlbear mother lets out a roar as her child is freed from the trap. The child and mother reconcile as the mother licks the child's wounds. She looks upon your party and gives her version of a bow of appreciation. With a grunt, the duo heads off into the woods.}
Depending on the state of the hunters, the nearby village will try to hunt the owlbear again on a later date or ignore the owlbear altogether.

entries.name: Alternate Solutions
entries: The party can negotiate with the villagers as well -they are amenable to reason as long as their primary goal, feeding their village, is met. Should the party provide food or resources to obtain food, the hunters will release the {@b Owlbear Cub} and not harm the {@b Owlbear Mother}.

entries.name: Possible Story Continuations (Optional)
entries: If you wish to continue any story elements with the owlbear or hunters:

items: The hunters who survived want revenge for what the party did and hire a bounty hunter to track them down and bring them to justice.
During an adventure, the same owlbear and cub will show up during combat to assist the party in thanks.

entries.name: Seeding the Feather of Nature's Might (If Owlbear Mother is Dead)
entries: {@i As the mother falls to the forest floor, nearby trees begin to shake, and their leaves fall onto the onto the recently slain Owlbear. One of her feathers shines brighter than the others - a token of your recently slain quarry. Could this be a token of good luck?}

entries.name: Reward: Seeding the Feather of Nature's Might (If Owlbear Mother is Alive)
entries: {@i The owlbear mother bows in a sign of respect. Its baby gives a cry of happiness, and the mother shakes her head, letting loose a feather. The baby grabs the feather in its mouth, runs up to you, and drops it at your feet. The duo goes back into the woods, leaving behind a token of good luck.}

entries.name: Overview
entries: This is a mystery encounter where the party will investigate a local disturbance. After a lot of build up and false leads, they find out at the end that the cause of the disturbance is a fan favorite - a Mimic!

The local {@b Bird Keeper's} shop is having issues with something sneaking in and taking his birds. The shopkeeper blames the local cats, but does not know if they are the true culprit or even how they are getting inside.

The shopkeeper will hire adventurers to find out what is taking his birds through a night-long stakeout. The shopkeeper will also lay down a few ground rules, such as "do not break anything in my shop."
This encounter will test the party's detective skills, create a fun air of mystery, and can seed future adventures.

items: The adventurers read a job board posting about a shopkeeper needing help protecting their storefront at night.
The adventurers hear a rumor about a shopkeeper kidnapping a bunch of cats from the streets, making them stand trial, and accusing them of heinous crimes.
Adventurers see a help wanted sign about finding missing birds and to contact the shopkeeper for more information.

entries.name: Creating Suspense
entries: Use the element of mystery and red herrings to make your players second guess everything. As stated earlier, the shopkeeper will not pay the adventurers if his shop is trashed, so they will have to be smart about how they investigate, and the {@b Mimic} has to be smart about what it eats and when.

entries.name: Potential Mimic Items
entries: The mimic itself can be any object in the store. Below are some suggestions, or you can roll a d6 and pick the corresponding item:

entries.name: Imitative Predators (As Per the Monster Manual 5e)
entries: Mimics can alter their outward texture to resemble wood, stone, and other basic materials. They have evolved to assume the appearance of objects that other creatures are likely to come into contact with. A mimic in its altered form is nearly unrecognizable until potential prey blunders into its reach, whereupon the monster sprouts pseudopods and attacks.

When the mimic changes shape, it excretes an adhesive that helps it seize prey and weapons that touch it. The adhesive is absorbed when the mimic assumes its amorphous form and in parts the mimic uses to move itself.

entries.name: Shopkeeper's Beliefs
entries: If questioned further, the Shopkeeper believes the following things:

items: The culprit is most likely a cat, but he does not know how it is getting inside.
There are no signs of breaking and entering
He has rivals in town, such as the old lady opening a competing pet store.
He did not pay a group of thugs protection money, so they might be stealing birds in response.

entries.name: The Stakeout and Red Herrings
entries: To add some suspense, we recommend utilizing some {@b Red Herrings} during the overnight stakeout. Having a Red Herring show up every 2 hours can cause the party to be on edge and question everything.
Below is a list of possible Red Herrings that can show up or happen. Roll a d6 or pick multiple ones you like:

rows: One of the exotic birds starts repeating, "He's gonna getcha, he's gonna getcha!"
There is a loud crash inside the shop, and the party hears something ghostlike.
If a party member looks outside, they can notice a cat jumping between buildings, landing on the roof adjacent to the shop.
A guard does several patrols by this shop, taking extra time and looking extra suspicious at this shop in particular.
A group of children run by the store, giggling as they pass.
An old lady with a large cart stands outside, staring at the store.

entries.name: Introduction
entries: {@i Upon your arrival at the shop, you see and hear many various species of birds in many different cages. The more colorful and exotic birds are in the back of the shop. As you look around, a gnome shopkeeper approaches and explains his situation.}

{@i "Every morning when I come to open the shop, I find feathers on the floor, and one of my birds is missing! I think it's the local cats sneaking in and eating my birds; I want you to stay the night and catch the cat in the act!  Well, maybe it's a cat. But that's where you come in! You catch the bird thief, and I will reward you. Do not trash my store, or you will be sorry!"}

entries.name: The Reveal
entries: At some point during the stakeout, the Mimic will go for a snack, ideally after a red herring or two. The mimic will go for a sneak attack against a bird OR a party member that is by themselves.

{@b If the mimic attacks a bird.} Everyone inside the shop can make a Perception check against the Mimic's Stealth (Mimic has +5), if they are outside the store, they make the check with disadvantage.
If they succeed the check, they will see a tongue come from wherever the mimic is pretending to be and that one of the birds is missing.
If no one in the party succeeds on the check, in time they will notice that one of the birds is gone.
If the party examines the object the mimic is pretending to be, the mimic will feel threatened and strike!

entries.name: Combat
entries: The mimic is a creature that is attacking to eat, so it will go after the nearest party member. The mimic will constantly try to grapple people in order to bite down on them. The mimic will fight to the death.

entries.name: Conclusion
entries: After the mimic has been defeated, and proof is shown to the shopkeeper that a mimic was eating the birds, the shopkeeper will be very shocked but grateful to the party. He will reward them with a {@item Figurine of a Silver Cockatiel|LPPDiBBaE}.

entries.name: Story Continuation Ideas
entries: The death of this mimic leads to more shopkeepers finding out that more mimics are hidden among the shops. They believe someone is planting mimics around the city.
This mimic is simple scout for something bigger that is preparing to consume the entire town.
An elder evil is placing small creatures around the town to test the defenses before it challenges the full might of the town.

entries.name: Reward: Seeding The Figurine of a Silver Cockatiel
entries: {@i The shop owner is impressed with your detective skills. He goes over to a locked cabinet in the back of the store and returns with a small bird figurine. "This is a magical figurine that can come to life. All you gotta do is say a bird's favorite snack. Cracker!" Upon uttering the word of power, the figurine glows, and with a bright flash the figurine becomes live Cockatiel. The bird flies to the nearest party member's shoulder and waits for instructions.}

entries.name: Overview
entries: A classic fight, this battle will test the adventurer’s mettle as they face off with a small horde of goblins, a large ogre, and a deadly mystic that is filling the goblins with zealous fervor. The party will need to use smart positioning and not overextend themselves deep into the goblin ranks in order to live through this fight.

entries.name: Fight Mechanics
entries: The adventurers will be fighting at the entrance to a temple or other building and will have cover from the approaching goblins. The goblins will need to cover ground quickly to reach the adventurers, which the party can fully leverage to their advantage. The large ogre is their shock trooper and will need to either be crowd controlled or killed quickly before it begins to crush the party. The mystic’s buffs are all concentration spells and can be broken somewhat easily.
The goblins know they outnumber the party and will use that to their advantage. There are the following enemies: 1 ogre, 1 hobgoblin, 1 goblin mystic, 5 goblin warriors, and 4 goblin archers.

entries.name: DM Note
entries: Feel free to increase or decrease the amount of goblins due to your party’s strength. This fight is meant to be a challenge, not to kill them outright!

entries.name: Before the Assault
entries: {@i You hear the horns of war off in the distance as a pack of goblins scurry towards your position. A large ogre is at the head of the pack, while two other figures seem to be in charge. You will have some time to plan your defense against the oncoming onslaught. Two worn down barricades look quite defensible and contain some old equipment from a previous battle.}

entries.name: Wooden Barricades
entries: These barricades provide the party with 3/4 cover (+5 AC)
The barricades have the following stats:

items: AC 10
10 hp
Resistant to piercing and slashing damage
Vulnerable to fire damage
Immune to Psychic damage
Difficult terrain

entries.name: Wooden Barricades
entries: The barricades have the following items in them:

items: Grease Bottle (Range 20/60, When thrown, does the effect of the {@spell grease|PHB} spell)
{@item Caltrops (bag of 20)|PHB}
{@item Alchemist's Fire (flask)|PHB}
{@item Short Bow|PHB}
{@item Light Crossbow|PHB}
{@item Arrows (20)|PHB}
{@item Crossbow Bolts (20)|PHB

entries.name: Stairs
entries: The stairs are difficult terrain.

entries.name: Fountain
entries: The fountain provides 1/2 cover (+2 AC) and can be utilized by the goblin archers and/or mystic.

entries.name: Trees
entries: Light Cover

entries.name: Enemy Behavior
entries: The enemy behavior is very straightforward, but keep these in mind:

items: The {@creature Ogre|LPPDiBBaE} will rush the party and either attempt to break down the barricades or engage in combat with the closest enemy.
The {@creature Hobgoblin|LPPDiBBaE} warrior will follow the Ogre and act as a second shock trooper.
The {@creature Goblin Mystic|LPPDiBBaE} will try to keep {@spell Bless|PHB} up at all times on at least 3 goblins.
The {@creature Goblin Warrior|LPPDiBBaE|Goblin Fighters} will try to rush the party.
The {@creature Goblin Archer|LPPDiBBaE}s will take up positions in the courtyard and attempt to shoot the easiest target available to them (ones not in cover).

entries.name: Conclusion
entries: {@i As the final foe gives its death rattle, you feel a sense of relief as the horns of war are silenced. They may have had the numbers, but you had bravery and tactics on your side. But goblins usually come back... and in greater numbers.}

items: The goblins were just a small raiding party. A new warlord has arisen and is uniting all of the goblin tribes under one banner.
The goblins were working for an evil, local noble who is trying to remove specific nearby villages that oppose his new laws.
After news of the defeat, other goblin leaders believe the party is the next big thing to hunt. Stronger warbands begin to hunt the party to claim their heads as trophies.

entries.name: Reward: Seeding the Club of the Mind Goblin
entries: {@i As the battle concludes, the ogre’s pack overflows with random junk and trinkets. But one item seems to shine more than the others; a very fine-crafted wooden greatclub emerges from the bag. Many nails appear to have been haphazardly added with some rudimentary magic. A snickering goblin is drawn on its handle.}

entries.title: Map of Goblins at the Gates
entries.altText: Map of Goblins at the Gates
entries.mapName: Map of Goblins at the Gates

entries.name: Overview
entries: This fight is against two {@b Half-Dragon Twins} on a bridge, whose special ability is their {@b Draconic Bond}. This bond allows them to buff one another and also resurrect a fallen twin as a reaction, meaning that the party will need to down them either simultaneously, split them up so that the bond is not in effect, utilize crowd control abilities that shut down reactions, or kill them fast enough that one twin cannot resurrect the other.
The bridge they are fighting on stands over a fiery pit, adding dramatic tension and more danger to the encounter. One twin is more martially-oriented while the other is a spellcaster.

items: While traveling, the adventurers come across the twins on a bridge. The twins require a toll to pass, and will attack if this is not paid.
Rumors circulate from the local mage’s guild of bonded individuals causing chaos on the roads.
A silver dragon has hired adventurers to kill these abnormal twins. The silver dragon believes their special bond can cause chaos in the future if not stopped.

entries.name: Fight Location: Bridge
entries: The bridge makes AOE spells more effective as there is not a lot of room to move, which can be beneficial when attempting to down the twins simultaneously. However, it also means that the spellcasting twin also benefits from the smaller area.

entries.name: Fiery Pit
entries: The pit is 20 feet below the bridge, and although the {@b Draconic Twins} have fire resistance, they can still take damage in the pit.

If a creature is within the pit at the start of their turn or when they first enter the pit, they must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 5d8} fire damage, half upon a successful save. All flammable objects that are worn on the creature will ignite.

entries.name: Draconic Bond
entries: The {@b Half-Dragon Twins} have a draconic bond that was formed at birth from divine magic. The bond exists while they are within 30 feet of each other, and has the following effects:

items: {@b Shared Advantage.} If either Twin has advantage for any reason, then both have advantage.
{@b Bonus Armor.} Each twin has a +2 bonus to AC while within 30 ft of the other twin.
{@b Draconic Lifeline (reaction).} If a twin drops to 0 hp while within 30 ft of their twin, the healthy twin can use their reaction to heal the injured twin for {@dice 5d8} hp.

{@b (Optional) Divine Rage.} If Draconic Bond is dispelled, the Twins will gain this effect. Sword Twin’s multiattack goes up to 4 Longsword Attacks (Up from 2) and the Magic Twin gains the ability to use {@spell Fire Burst|PHB} 6 times (up from 3).

entries.name: Draconic Lifeline Description
entries: {@i A magical tether binds the two Dragon Twins, visibly giving each one power from the other. The closer they are, the brighter the tether appears. The farther they go apart, the dimmer the tether...}

entries.name: (Optional) Dispelling Draconic Bond
entries: If you choose to have Draconic Bond be dispellable, read the following once it is dispelled.
{@b Note:} The twins will gain {@b Divine Rage} if Draconic Bond is dispelled.
{@i The magical tether between the twins dissipates, and they look at each other in confusion. That confusion quickly turns to rage as they fall upon you with violent desperation.}

entries.name: The Twin’s Combat Behavior
entries: The twins both understand their {@b Draconic Bond} and will use it to the fullest. They will attempt to stay within 30 ft of each other to keep the bond going, and if one is forcefully moved outside of that range, they will quickly re-group to restore the bond.
Should the battle not go their way, the Twins will attempt to flee combat as they value their lives and bond more than defeating the party.

The mage has {@spell telekinesis|PHB}, which can be used to pull the sword twin out of combat if things are getting too dangerous for them. The mage can also use {@spell telekinesis|PHB} to throw party members off the bridge.

entries.name: (Optional) Giving the Party Preliminary Information About the Bond
entries: If the hook that you use involves getting the bounty/quest from someone to kill the twins, then the quest giver will mention that the bond between the twins gives them powers, including resurrection. One twin cannot die while the other lives as what appears to be divine magic brings one back from the brink of death.
They have not found a way to solve this problem, but a survivor of the twins did mention that the bond seemed weaker the farther apart the twins were from one another.
Giving the adventurers a heads up about the bond can be helpful, or they can learn about it during the fight through arcana checks if you wish to make the encounter more difficult.

entries.name: Introduction
entries: {@i Upon walking onto the stone bridge, you feel the heat from the fiery pit below. At the end of the bridge stands two red half-dragon individuals. They look extremely similar to one another - almost identical. A glowing, orange aura permeates from each of their hearts. One is equipped in ornate full plate with two swords drawn. The other wears robes, fire swirling around their hands. The armored half-dragon points a sword in your direction and shouts, "drop everything you have if you value your life."}
{@i What do you do?}
The half-dragons will allow the party to pass should they drop all of their valuables.

entries.name: First Use of Draconic Lifeline
entries: The first time {@i Draconic Lifeline} is used, read the following:

{@i As the twin hits the ground, their life force leaves their body. The orange-glowing hearts from both twins begin to beat rapidly, one slower than the other. However, the hearts begin beating in sync. A draconic roar echoes through the skies as a pillar of flame hits the downed twin. Its eyes open, and the twin comes back to life in an instant. They are ready for battle once more.}

entries.name: Conclusion
entries: {@i As the twins are defeated, their bodies turn to ash. A draconic roar echoes through the skies once again. The fiery pit extinguishes itself, and smoke erupts from the charred ground. A gust of wind carries away the ashes of the twins.}

items: The hearts the twins had was just the start. The gods of an evil-aligned dragon is using these twins as a starting point to create an entire army of draconic-bonded soldiers.

The draconic hearts survived the twin’s death, and mages believe they can be used to create something greater. However, there are differing mage factions at odds with one another, arguing about what should be done with the hearts and how.

entries.name: Reward: Seeding the Draconic Scimitar of Speed and Draconic Pendant of Power
entries: {@i While the twin’s bodies have dissipated into the wind, some of their equipment remains. A ruby dragon hilt decorates a scimitar that vibrates with energy. Nearby, a ruby pendant swirls with power, as if wanting to be found...}

entries.name: Overview
entries: In this battle, the party will encounter a mounted {@b Duergar} atop a {@b Rust Monster}. This {@b Rider} is in a graveyard stealing corpses for nefarious reasons. Combat wise, the {@b Rust Monster} is fast, it debuffs metal objects, and the {@b Duergar} rider hits hard in melee. The party will need to utilize the graveyard’s terrain to avoid the Rust Monster’s charging attacks.

entries.name: Potential Hooks
entries: This battle takes place in a {@b graveyard} and can be incorporated onto your table by any of the following hooks. Feel free to add your own as you see fit:

items: Random graveyard encounter.
A concerned citizen hires the party to stop whomever is digging up corpses in the middle of the night.
Hunters have sighted an odd insect-like creature and want it dead. The creature has been spotted prowling graveyards at night.

entries.name: Introduction
entries: {@i As you approach the graveyard, the sound of digging echoes through the still night. As you get closer, you see a massive, bug-like creature next to an open grave. The monster does not seem bothered by the weight of its numerous saddlebags. A duergar climbs out of the grave, and with a grunt, he throws a body on top of the creature.}
A DC 12 Nature or Arcana check will recognize the creature as a {@b Rust Monster}.

The {@b Duergar Rider} will not immediately notice the party. Should the party make their presence known, the Duergar will threaten the party to leave. If they do not, he will jump on the {@b Mutated Rust Monster} and attack!

entries.name: Fight Overview and Mechanics
entries: The {@b Duergar} begins the fight mounted upon the {@b Mutated Rust Monster} and will utilize its speed to deliver devastating charging attacks or keep at range and throw javelins. He will increase his size with Enlarge to deal more damage while the {@b Rust Monster} destroys metal in melee.

items: {@b Open Graves and Fences.} Littering the graveyard are many open graves and fences, which will limit the rider’s movement. The players can use this to their advantage to dodge charging attacks and to stay out of melee with the {@b Mutated Rust Monster} (if so desired). The rider is intelligent enough to know which directions to travel to allow his mount to get the most out of its abilities. If any fences get in his way, the Rust Monster will do what it does best and rust them away.

{@b Open Graves:} The {@b Rust Monster} cannot move through {@b Open Graves} naturally. It can, however, {@b jump} over an open grave. If it jumps, it cannot use its charging attack. Players will need to jump over {@b Open Graves} as well. Players can go inside the open grave, but it is not recommended!

{@b Fences:} The fences are {@b difficult terrain}, meaning the {@b Rust Monster} cannot use its charge ability when going through them. The Rust Monster can, however, remove fences from the battlefield by rusting them away using its {@b antennae action}.
{@b Mausoleum:} The Mausoleum is 10 ft tall. The Rust Monster can Jump to get on top of it.

entries: {@b Difficult Terrain:} You move at half speed in difficult terrain, moving 1 foot in difficult terrain costs 2 feet of speed, so you can cover only half the normal distance in a minute, an hour, or a day.
{@b Mounted Rider.} The Rust monster and Rider work on the rider’s initiative.

{@b Alternate Initiative Option.} You can have the Rust Monster and Rider go on separate initiatives to liven up the encounter. However, this may impede one or the other as they cannot work in tandem.
The Rust Monster understands all commands from the rider and will never flee as it is a war-trained mount.

entries.name: Combat Behavior and Tactics
entries: The Rider will jump on his rust monster to engage the party. The {@b Rust Monster} and {@b Rider} will always first attempt to get a 15ft charge on the party for additional damage.

If the party is splitting up, the {@b Duergar} can dismount to engage half of the party while the {@b Rust Monster} engages the other. The rider can give verbal commands to the rust monster as a {@b free action}, so the rider can regroup with the {@b Rust Monster} if things are getting dicey.

If the {@b Duergar} dies, the {@b Rust Monster} will go into a frenzy and fight to the death. However, the {@b Duergar} values his life and will not fight to the death. Should the {@b Duergar} feel outmatched, he will flee, utilizing his invisibility and/or the {@b Rust Monster’s} speed.

entries.name: Conclusion
entries: This fight can seed multiple open-ended questions and plot into your storyline, or it can simply be a one-off encounter. Below are some ideas you can use to add more story elements after the fight.

items: {@b Necromancer Contract.} Upon searching the Duergar, you find that he was contracted by a Necromancer to collect certain bodies. The contract has a drop-off location for the bodies, which is near the Necromancer’s Lair.

{@b Mutated Rust Monster - Endangered?} This species of Rust Monster has been considered extinct for many years. This one can be one that has survived, is a new breed, or was created. Either way, it is a threat.
{@b Cult Hideout.} By examining the Duergar, the party finds a holy symbol of an evil god along with a map, indicating potential cult activity along with a hideout.

entries.name: Reward: Seeding the Exotic Saddle
entries: {@i After examining the Rust Monster’s corpse, you notice everything on him is attached to one massive saddle. When removed, the saddle shrinks into to generic horse saddle size. It feels much lighter than expected.}

entries.name: Overview
entries: This encounter features a haunted house filled with many objects that can be possessed by the local poltergeist, the {@b Master of the House} (also referred to as {@b ghost} and {@b Master). There are multiple ways to do this encounter, including RP elements using Charisma-based skills, calming the evil spirit, or simply battling it.

entries.name: Potential Locations and Hooks
entries: This encounter is pretty flexible, but here are some ideas as to locations you can use:

items: Random house in the middle of a city, town, or village.
A notable NPC’s House.
Abandoned house in a forest.
A job posting from an NPC that needs help.
{@b A Landlord’s Problem:} Provided hook.

entries: Tying this encounter into an NPC that the party knows can be helpful, as they explain they have been hearing noises and witnessing objects flying across the room at random hours of the day.

entries.name: A Landlord’s Problem
entries: An elf landlord by the name of {@b Meorise Heimys} recently purchased a house for cheap and wants to renovate the house to sell. When she was examining the property, she kept hearing ghostly wails, saw objects being thrown against the wall, and when the clock struck 3am she heard the words, "GET OUT OF MY HOUSE" echoing through the halls.

She is hiring a group of adventurers to deal with this situation because haunted houses don’t sell. She will offer the party a reward to clear out the ghost or even give them a discount on the property should they want to buy it. She will need confirmation that the ghost is gone.

entries.name: Introduction
entries: {@i As you approach the old manor, many old vines envelop the walls outside of the building. With the key to the front door in your pocket, you approach the door, only for it to open on its own. You feel a cold shiver go up your spine as if the house is welcoming you inside.}

entries.name: Using An Exploration Start
entries: Should the party decide to explore the house, the {@b Master of the House} will possess an object in a room of your choosing, waiting for the right time to strike. The Master of the House’s goal is to get the party to leave the house at first through warnings, and later by force. Let your inner horror director shine, but here are some examples:

items: Creaky hallways and sounds.
Windows opening and closing.
Whispering "get out of my house."

entries: From here, the players have many options in front of them. Should they anger the spirit or linger too long, it will attack. However, they do have the option to put the spirit to rest peacefully.

entries.name: Peacefully Putting the Spirit to Rest
entries: the {@b Master of the House} is an old spirit that doesn't want anyone to live in its house as it believes it is still alive. However, it can be convinced that it has passed on or lied to creatively to get it to leave. Increase or decrease the DCs based on roleplay or hostility against the ghost.

items: {@b DC 15 Persuasion Check} - To convince the ghost that it is dead.
{@b DC 18 Persuasion Check} - To convince the ghost that he does not own the house any more.
{@b DC 20 Persuasion Check} - To let go, not be scared of the afterlife, and to be at peace.

entries.name: Using a Combat Start
entries: Should you want the combat to start immediately, the {@b Master of the House} can possess the Chandelier or another object and instantly attack the party. However, this option can limit the "putting to rest peacefully" option.

entries.name: Combat Overview
entries: The Master of the House has one main goal - to get the adventurers out of the house by any means necessary, but mainly by possessing objects and attacking the party as per {@b Object Possession}.

During the battle, if the {@b Master of the House} is feeling cornered or overwhelmed, it will escape to a different room. The Master of the House can go through walls as per its {@b Incorporeal Movement}. You can use this ability to show the players the whole house as well as the different effects of the {@b possessed objects}.
If a character goes outside, the ghost will not pursue. Should the players attack the ghost from the outside, the ghost will simply move to another part of the house where it cannot be shot.

entries.name: Object Possession
entries: the {@b Master of the House} has an ability that allows him to possess objects and use the object as a "Host." The object he possesses confers abilities based on the object’s size. The possession is not obvious, so if the {@b Master of the House} can get to another room and possess something without the characters noticing, it can create fear as the party does not know which items they can trust. Make them question everything!

There is a large selection of objects the ghost can possess, each with their own unique abilities. Below are just some examples of objects in the specific rooms of the house that can be possessed along with their stats and abilities. Just make sure when they enter a new room, you describe the objects inside so that the players have an idea of what the {@b Master} can possess.

All objects have the following AC, HP, Basic Stats, and condition immunities. Some of these are as per the {@b {@spell Animate Object|PHB}} spell. All objects have a speed, but if the object doesn't make sense to move then you can choose to not have it move. For example: A door.

entries.name: Object "Death."
entries: When an object reaches 0 hp, the {@b Master of the House} appears within 5ft of the object, and any leftover damage from the object goes to the Master. On his turn, he will fight the party or possess another object.

entries.name: Possessing Objects As Per Animate Object
entries: The Master can possess anything within the house. Use these statlines depending on what you have him possess.

items: {@creature Animate Object (Tiny)|LPPDiBBaE}
{@creature Animate Object (Small)|LPPDiBBaE}
{@creature Animate Object (Medium)|LPPDiBBaE}
{@creature Animate Object (Large)|LPPDiBBaE}
{@creature Animate Object (Huge)|LPPDiBBaE}

entries.name: If the Master of the House is Destroyed
entries: {@i As you deliver the final blow to the spirit, the house shakes and the dust settles. The light from the Master of the House slowly fades into the abyss as his spirit dissipates. The light from outside shines brighter into the house as it has been cleared of any unwanted occupants.}

entries.name: If the Master of the House is Put to Rest
entries: {@i The Master looks at all of you apologetically. He gives a small bow and says, “I apologize if I have done anything to cause harm. I wasn't myself, and you all have shown me that. Thank you.” A bright, radiant light blinds everyone in the room and fades slowly. The spirit is gone. The formerly dim and uninviting atmosphere lifts as the house now seems welcoming and a full of light.}

items: The Master of the House wasn’t originally haunting this house. Someone captured him and placed him there to increase house prices.
More ghosts are appearing as something is preventing  ghosts in the local area from being put to rest. Some people say it is a spirit board gone wrong, and that it must be found!
The Master of the House’s former spouse is furious that the party put him to rest and wants revenge in some form.

entries.name: Reward: Seeding the Ghostly Rod of Rulership

items: {@i As the ghost disappears in a flash of light, a large thud is heard as a rod falls from where the ghost once stood. The rod is ethereal and seems to be pulsing with magic.}
A- Foyer (Front Door)
B- Kitchen
C- Parlor Room
D- Dining Room
E-Drawing Room
F- Main Hallway
G- Guest Bedroom
H- Master Bedroom
I- Bathroom

entries.name: Rewards: Seeding the Breastplate of Honor
Rewards: Seeding the Breastplate of Honor

item.name: Breastplate of Honor
entries: This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.

If the wearer misses an attack roll with a weapon, they can gain focus and try again. The wearer immediately makes another against the same target, but this time with advantage. After this effect is used, it cannot be used again until after a short or long rest.

item.name: Draconic Rat Egg
entries: This small rat egg weighs about 1 pound. It has a red tint to the egg and is warm to the touch. If the egg is kept in a warm environment of at least 85 Degrees Fahrenheit for 8-10 days, the egg will hatch into a baby Draconic Rat with 1 HP. The party can raise it as a pet or do whatever they want with this creature once it hatches.

The Draconic Rat becomes an adult after 1 month. During that time, small wings will start to grow from the rat, and it will begin to randomly breathe fire showing its growth. The average draconic rat lives 5-6 years.
Below are the stat blocks for the Draconic Rat Companion that through its various stages of life.

items: {@creature Baby Draconic Rat|LPPDiBBaE}
{@creature Adult Draconic Rat|LPPDiBBaE}

item.name: Feather of Nature's Might
entries: This feather has 10 charges. Once this feather has 0 charges, the feather becomes a normal Owlbear feather and loses all magical properties:

items: By spending 1 charge, you automatically are considered to have rolled a natural 20 on a single {@skill Animal Handling} Check.
By spending 1 charge, you may add {@dice 2d8} damage to a single unarmed attack, as you notice your hand becomes an Owlbear claw for a single swing.
By spending 1 charge, you may cast {@spell Speak with Animals|PHB}, but with an increased duration of 1 day.
By spending 1 charge, the might of the trees is imbued within you. You instantly restore {@dice 2d8} HP.

item.name: Figurine of a Silver Cockatiel
entries: A figurine of wondrous power is a statuette of a beast small enough to fit in a pocket. If you use an action to speak the command word and throw the figurine to a point on the ground within 60 feet of you, the figurine becomes a living creature. If the space where the creature would appear is occupied by other creatures or objects, or if there isn't enough space for the creature, the figurine doesn't become a creature.
The creature is friendly to you and your companions. It understands your languages and obeys your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions.

The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to speak the command word again while touching it. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified in the figurine's description.

This silver statuette is carved in the likeness of a Cockatiel. It can become a {@creature Cockatiel|LPPDiBBaE} for up to 12 hours. Once it has been used, it cannot be used again until 1 day has passed.

item.name: Club of the Mind Goblin
entries: You gain +1 to attack rolls and damage rolls while using this Greatclub.
When you hit with a melee attack using this Greatclub and reduce the target to 0 hit points, you gain {@dice 2d4} temporary hit points.
If attuned by a Goblinoid, you gain the following ability:

entries.name: Battle Cry
entries: As an action, each creature of the wielder's choice that is within 30 feet of it, can hear it, and is not already affected by Battle Cry, gains advantage on attack rolls until the start of the wielder’s next turn. The wielder then can make one attack as a bonus action. After this ability is used, it cannot be used again until the user takes a short or long rest.

item.name: Exotic Riding Saddle
entries: This magical saddle can fit any beast or monstrosity it is given to. The saddle itself contains 5 saddlebags that can hold up to 50 pounds each for a total of 250 pounds. If the creature wearing the saddle is willing and controlled, the rider gains advantage on any riding-related checks while the saddle is attached to the mount.

If the beast or monstrosity is unwilling to wear the saddle, upon placement of the saddle, the beast or monstrosity must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw against {@spell Charm Monster|PHB}. The creature attempting to place the saddle is considered the caster for all intents and purposes.
After one hour, the charm monster effect ends. This effect can only be used once every 24 hours.

item.name: Ghostly Rod of Rulership
entries: You can use an action to present the rod and command obedience from each creature of your choice that you can see within 120 feet of you. Each target must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for 8 hours. While charmed in this way, the creature regards you as its trusted leader. If harmed by you or your companions, or commanded to do something contrary to its nature, a target ceases to be charmed in this way. The rod can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Telekinetic Thrust
entries: As an Action, the wielder targets a creature or unattended object within 30 feet of it. A creature must be Medium or smaller to be affected by this magic, and an object can weigh up to 150 pounds.

items: {@b If the target is a creature}, the rod wielder makes a Charisma check contested by the target’s Strength check. If the wielder wins the contest, the wielder hurls the target up to 30ft in any direction, including upward. If the target then comes into contact with a hard surface or heavy object, the target takes {@dice 1d6} damage per 10ft moved.

{@b If the target is an object} that isn’t being worn or carried, the rod wielder hurls it up to 30ft in any direction. The wielder can use the object as a ranged weapon, attacking one creature along the object’s path and dealing 15 ({@dice 3d10}) bludgeoning damage on a hit.

entries: The wielder can use this ability a number of times equal to their Charisma modifier. When you finish a long rest, you regain all expended uses.
